title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation · Graph Lens: Gita Rebase Replay Model
summary: A stacked change series must move between release bases, preserve selected merges, reorder dependent commits, and satisfy a review policy without losing authorship or test evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita rebase replay model increment in rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
Record conflict cause, chosen invariant, rerere provenance, changed-case tests, and abort or continuation evidence for future integrators.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Rebase Replay Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G25 is a theory in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Rebase Replay Model contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.

content.text: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology. Apply that move specifically to Gita Rebase Replay Model while producing rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G25 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass. Competency probe: Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic. A defensible solution applies Gita Rebase Replay Model, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof.

content.code:
# Worked focus: gita-rebase-replay-model — Model rebase as commit selection, temporary reset, ordered replay, new object creation, and ref movement rather than branch movement magic.
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity
git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
# expected: range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries
# observation: four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review
# changed-case: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass
# transfer-limit: coordinate real shared-history rewrites and publish with an explicit lease expectation
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Rebase Replay Model evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-rebase-replay-model-19dfwn" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Rebase Replay Model into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-rebase-replay-model-19dfwn
# scenario-key: trace-19dfwn probe-nwfd91 boundary-dfwn19 owner-19dfwnx fault-19dfwnz
# before-ref: main=a11ce19 candidate=b00b1wn
# hypothesis: the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity
git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
# expected: range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries
# observation: four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review
# changed-case: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge
# after-ref: main=a11ce19 candidate=c0ffeen
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass
# transfer-limit: coordinate real shared-history rewrites and publish with an explicit lease expectation
